vulnerability.title: Uninstall vsftpd Package
vulnerability.description: The vsftpd package can be removed with the following command: $ sudo dnf remove vsftpd
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # CAUTION: This remediation script will remove vsftpd # from the system, and may remove any packages # that depend on vsftpd. Execute this # remediation AFTER testing on a non-production # system! if rpm -q --quiet "vsftpd" ; then dnf remove -y --noautoremove "vsftpd" fi

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only removes the vsftpd package using a standard package manager command. This does not affect system boot, authentication, or critical services, and the policy explicitly allows package removals to be classified as safe. The only risk is removal of dependent packages, which can be mitigated by testing on a non‑production system.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: networking affected; Potential removal of packages that depend on vsftpd; Loss of FTP service functionality
